evidence: Image and video lightbox render-model assembly is duplicated almost field-for-field (lightboxVariants/lightboxStateValue/workflowBar/workflowControls/layoutProps) in src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx:550-842 and src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx:176-520.

useSegmentSlotModeData memoization depends on whole object identities (`props`, `state`) while `state` is recreated each render in useSegmentSlotMode, making the expensive useMemo effectively recompute every render (src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useSegmentSlotMode.ts:414-421, 459-468).

useInlineEditState constructs a very large non-memoized return graph (`imageEditValue` plus nested state groups) and InlineEditView passes that whole object through multiple subcomponents/providers, increasing incidental rerender coupling (src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts:344-490, src/tools/edit-images/components/InlineEditView.tsx:105-188, 217-258).

Task creation modules repeat large param bags and manual field mapping/validation paths instead of composing shared task-shape primitives, increasing local indirection and drift risk (src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts:110-218, src/shared/lib/tasks/magicEdit.ts:24-129, src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts:36-141).

Holistic structure shows `src/shared/hooks/` is a flat high-load directory (`file_count: 81`) while domain subfolders already exist (`billing/`, `clipboard/`, `responsive/`, `theme/`), indicating boundary drift rather than intentional flat layout.

`src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useCopyToClipboard.test.ts` targets `../clipboard/useCopyToClipboard`, while a colocated domain test already exists at `src/shared/hooks/clipboard/__tests__/useCopyToClipboard.test.ts`.

`src/shared/components/BatchDropZone.test.tsx` is a root-level test straggler, while the component and colocated test live under `src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/components/` and `.../__tests__/BatchDropZone.test.tsx`.

`src/shared/hooks/useCredits.ts` has a parallel implementation in `src/features/billing/hooks/useCredits.ts`, and both have near-duplicate test files, creating two package homes for the same domain API.

findings.summary: Image/video lightboxes duplicate large render-model builders with only minor deltas.
evidence: Both modules independently build `lightboxVariants`, `lightboxStateValue`, `workflowBar`, `workflowControls`, and `layoutProps` with near-identical key sets and dependency lists (Image: 550-842, Video: 176-520).
Both duplicate `handleNavigateToShotFromSelector` with the same minimal-shot adapter pattern (Image: 655-660, Video: 293-300).
The duplication sits in low-level assembly logic (object construction + dependency choreography), so small behavior changes require parallel edits in two files.
findings.suggestion: Extract shared lightbox render-model builders (e.g., `buildCommonVariantsModel`, `buildCommonWorkflowModel`, `buildCommonLayoutProps`) and keep only media-specific branches as strategy callbacks. This should collapse duplicated dependency arrays and make behavior changes single-source.

findings.summary: Object-bag dependencies invalidate memoization and hide rerender costs.
evidence: useSegmentSlotModeData uses `useMemo(..., [props, state, ...])` while `state` is newly allocated each render (`const state: SegmentSlotState = { ... }`), forcing recomputation of the large SegmentSlotModeData object (useSegmentSlotMode.ts:414-421, 459-468).
useInlineEditState allocates a very wide `imageEditValue` object and additional nested groups on every render with no memo partitioning (useInlineEditState.ts:344-490).

InlineEditView passes the whole `state` object into multiple component branches and providers (`InlineEditCanvas`, `InlineEditSidebar`, `ImageEditProvider`), amplifying reference churn downstream (InlineEditView.tsx:105-188, 217-258).
findings.suggestion: Replace bag-level dependencies with scalar dependencies in `useSegmentSlotModeData`, memoize stable slices in `useInlineEditState` (`useMemo`/`useCallback` per concern), and pass narrowed props to `InlineEditCanvas`/`InlineEditSidebar` so unchanged branches can stay referentially stable.

findings.summary: Task builders repeat oversized param interfaces and manual mapping logic.
evidence: imageGeneration defines separate single/batch param bags with overlapping fields and separate validators (imageGeneration.ts:110-218).
magicEdit repeats the same single/batch split and manually rehydrates single params from batch fields for validation (magicEdit.ts:24-129).
individualTravelSegment introduces another large task-specific bag plus custom validation accumulation and conversion helpers (individualTravelSegment.ts:36-141).
payloadBuilder continues manual `Record<string, unknown>` assembly with many conditionals for the same family, increasing divergence points (payloadBuilder.ts:115-210).
findings.suggestion: Introduce shared task-input primitives (base lineage/resolution/seed/loras contracts) and reusable validators/composers, then compose per-task deltas on top. Keep one canonical field mapping per concern (lineage, resolution, seed, loras) to eliminate manual repetition.

findings.summary: Legacy video-edit fields can be accepted then discarded without explicit validation
evidence: `hasLegacyVideoEditFields` treats `source_*` and `portions_to_regenerate` as meaningful legacy inputs (94-102), but `resolveVideoEditConfig` ignores them unless `video_edit_mode` is true (178-180).
The destructuring cleanup removes these legacy fields from `shared` before canonical return (232-246), so callers can pass partial legacy payloads that are neither honored nor rejected.
findings.suggestion: Add a strict guard: if any legacy video-edit fields are present while `video_edit_mode` is not true, throw a `TaskValidationError` with a migration hint. Keep acceptance behavior only for fully valid legacy mode inputs.

findings.summary: Variant-to-row adapter rewrites row identity to parent generation ID
evidence: `variantToGenerationRow` sets `id` and `generation_id` to the same `generationId` (95-100), while also storing original variant id only inside `params.variant_id` (108).
The `GenerationRow` contract is a persisted-row-based shape (`PersistedGenerationRow` + derived fields), where `id` is expected to remain row identity rather than a convenience alias.
findings.suggestion: Preserve unique row identity: set `id` to `media.id`, set `generation_id` to resolved parent generation ID, and only fall back when one is truly absent. Update adapter call sites/tests that currently rely on collapsed IDs.

findings.summary: Core task contracts keep overlapping legacy and canonical fields, increasing local branching
evidence: `TravelBetweenImagesTaskInput` combines modern params with legacy fields and redefines `structure_videos` to a legacy-enriched shape (344-352), so downstream code must reason about both schemas.

Legacy structure fields remain duplicated in multiple interfaces (`LegacyStructureVideoFields` and `LegacyTravelBetweenImagesTaskParams`), preserving parallel knobs for equivalent behavior (18-27, 33-47).
Join-clips compat path similarly accepts a large mixed bag of canonical, legacy path, and legacy video-edit fields (24-88), then resolves precedence with multiple helper branches.
findings.suggestion: Introduce explicit boundary DTOs: keep legacy acceptance only at ingress adapters, immediately normalize to one canonical internal type, and prevent legacy fields from appearing in shared/core task interfaces.

findings.summary: BatchDropZone test is duplicated across root shared/components and ShotImageManager subtree.
evidence: Both test files contain the same single assertion (`exports component`) with equivalent structure.
Root test path (`src/shared/components/BatchDropZone.test.tsx`) does not match component ownership; implementation lives in `ShotImageManager/components`.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg: 1) keep `src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/components/__tests__/BatchDropZone.test.tsx` as canonical and remove `src/shared/components/BatchDropZone.test.tsx`; 2) if any imports point to the root test/module path, rewrite to `ShotImageManager/components`; 3) validate with `rg -n "BatchDropZone\.test" src/shared/components` and `pnpm vitest src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/components/__tests__/BatchDropZone.test.tsx`.

findings.summary: Credits hook exists in both shared and feature billing packages, fragmenting ownership.
evidence: Two exported `useCredits` implementations exist with overlapping query/mutation responsibilities.
Two separate test suites validate equivalent `useCredits` behavior, confirming duplicated package surface rather than adapter-only split.
`useCredits` is billing-specific but is currently rooted under `src/shared/hooks/`, while feature billing package already exists.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg: 1) choose canonical location `src/features/billing/hooks/useCredits.ts`; 2) move callers from `@/shared/hooks/useCredits` to `@/features/billing/hooks/useCredits`; 3) keep a temporary compatibility re-export in shared (deprecated) if needed for rollout; 4) merge/delete duplicate tests after parity check; 5) validate with `rg -n "@/shared/hooks/useCredits|@/features/billing/hooks/useCredits" src`, `pnpm vitest src/features/billing/hooks/__tests__/useCredits.test.ts`, and `pnpm tsc --noEmit`.

findings.summary: Root `hooks/__tests__` contains tests for subpackage-owned hooks, blurring directory boundaries.
evidence: `useCopyToClipboard.test.ts` in root imports `../clipboard/useCopyToClipboard`, crossing into subpackage ownership from a flat test bucket.
`useContentResponsive.test.ts` in root validates responsive hook behavior (`useViewportResponsive`) instead of a root-owned hook.
Holistic profile shows `src/shared/hooks/` has 81 files, and `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/ -> src/shared/hooks/` is a high-volume coupling edge (80), signaling central test-bucket gravity.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg: 1) move root tests to colocated subpackage test folders (`clipboard/__tests__`, `responsive/__tests__`); 2) for any root-only legacy names, add one release-cycle alias file with deprecation comment, then remove; 3) enforce path convention (test mirrors implementation directory); 4) validate with `desloppify move src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useCopyToClipboard.test.ts src/shared/hooks/clipboard/__tests__/useCopyToClipboard.test.ts --dry-run`, `desloppify move src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useContentResponsive.test.ts src/shared/hooks/responsive/__tests__/useContentResponsive.test.ts --dry-run`, and `pnpm vitest src/shared/hooks/clipboard/__tests__/useCopyToClipboard.test.ts src/shared/hooks/responsive/__tests__/useContentResponsive.test.ts`.

findings.summary: Legacy Supabase facade remains centrally placed and widely consumed across non-integration layers.
evidence: README defines legacy facade as compatibility-only with removal target 2026-06-30 and CI gates, but `legacySupabaseFacade.ts` remains a heavy shared dependency (133 importers; `src/shared/hooks/ -> src/integrations/supabase/legacy/` edge count 36).
Current folder location under `integrations/supabase/legacy` is correct for ownership, but call-site distribution shows the compatibility boundary is functionally acting as a runtime API.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) add a stable runtime contract at `src/integrations/supabase/runtime/publicClient.ts` (target folder: runtime) and migrate direct consumers in `src/shared/hooks/` first, then `src/shared/components/`, then `src/tools/`; 2) keep `legacySupabaseFacade.ts` as a temporary forwarding shim only in `legacy/`; 3) run `rg "legacySupabaseFacade|getLegacySupabaseClient" src`, update imports in that order, then validate with `npm run check:legacy-supabase-usage && npm run check:contracts && npm run quality:check`.

findings.summary: Runtime error facade also exports governance policy constants, mixing operational and contract concerns.
evidence: `handleError.ts` exports both runtime handlers and governance metadata (`RUNTIME_ERROR_ALIAS_*`, budget helpers) from `src/shared/lib/governance/runtimeErrorAliasPolicy`.
This file is a hub (248 importers), so policy placement inside the runtime facade broadens policy coupling and makes the facade a mixed-responsibility import target.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) create `src/shared/lib/governance/contracts/runtimeErrorAliasContract.ts` (target folder: governance/contracts) and move re-exported policy symbols there; 2) keep `handleError.ts` limited to runtime behavior (`handleError`, `handleAndRethrow`); 3) migrate imports by first replacing policy imports from `handleError.ts`, then keep deprecated alias exports temporarily if needed; 4) run `rg "RUNTIME_ERROR_ALIAS_|getRuntimeErrorAliasImportBudget" src`, update imports, then validate with `npm run check:error-runtime-alias-usage && npm run check:contracts && npm run quality:check`.

findings.summary: Generation/shot type contracts are split between global `src/types` and domain-local type directories.
evidence: `src/types/generationAndShots.ts` is labeled canonical and heavily imported (205), while the codebase also maintains `src/domains/generation/types/` as a separate type package.

README migration guidance removed one shim (`@/types/shots`) but keeps canonical guidance centered on `@/types/generationAndShots`, reinforcing cross-root ownership split instead of converging on domain-owned contracts.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) establish `src/domains/generation/contracts/index.ts` as the long-term canonical type surface (target folder: domain contracts); 2) convert `src/types/generationAndShots.ts` into a deprecated compatibility barrel forwarding to domain contracts; 3) migrate imports in waves (`src/tools/travel-between-images` first, then `src/shared/hooks`, then `src/shared/components`); 4) run `rg "@/types/generationAndShots|@/types/(generationParams|generationMetadata|generationRow|shot)" src`, update imports, then validate with `npm run check:shots-shim-usage && npm run check:contracts && npm run quality:check`.
